finding: No coverage measurement anywhere: pyproject has no coverage config and CI runs bare `pytest` (no --cov). Coverage regressions (a new feature added with no tests) are invisible; "tests pass" is the only gate.
proposed_action: Add a dev dependency on coverage/pytest-cov and a documented `make cov` / pytest --cov invocation; optionally a non-gating coverage report in CI. Do NOT hard-gate a % (brittle) initially.

finding: Many TUI modal tests assert `isinstance(app.screen, XModal)` immediately after a single `pilot.pause()` with no mount-poll loop. This is the exact class of Windows-timing flake that already forced reruns (test_tui_clear_history was hardened; the pattern recurs at ~10 sites).
evidence: tests/test_tui.py:164,199,238,339,383,642 (assert isinstance(app.screen,...) right after one pause)
proposed_action: Add a small `await_screen(pilot, app, ModalCls)` helper that polls (like the existing 50x0.1s loops) and use it at every such assertion, so the modal-mount race is eliminated suite-wide.

finding: Critical destructive/transfer functions are well-covered on the happy path but have sizeable UNCOVERED branches (error/rollback/edge): _execute_move 63 missed lines, extract_and_import_project 59, db_delete_project_recursive 51, db_delete_session_recursive 42, cli_restore 29, db_delete_sessions_batch 25. These are LIVE/data-integrity surfaces where the error/rollback path is the risky one.
evidence: coverage json: ocman/cli.py missing lines mapped per-function (run record evidence.md)
proposed_action: Add targeted negative/rollback tests for the uncovered error branches of these functions (e.g. mid-operation failure -> rollback leaves state intact; partial-failure reporting), prioritizing the delete/import/move rollback paths.

finding: Remote-move / git-aware decision code (_gather_git_decisions 53 missed of 95; remote-move runbook branch in _execute_move) is largely untested; it prints a runbook and gathers git state, hard to exercise without a git+remote fixture.
proposed_action: Propose a testable seam: extract the pure decision logic from the git/IO so the branch selection can be unit-tested with fabricated git state; add tests for the runbook-text branch selection.

finding: TUI is the weakest-covered area: widgets/database.py 53%, app.py 65%. Interactive worker/error paths (background export failure, refresh guards) are largely untested beyond smoke.
proposed_action: Add focused async tests for the highest-risk TUI worker/error paths (esp. database.py mutating actions and the off-thread worker error handling), not a blanket coverage push.

finding: Perf/benchmark tests are skip-by-default (OCMAN_BENCHMARK=1) and therefore never run in CI, so a performance regression on the export/import/rebase hot paths would not be caught.
proposed_action: Propose either a tiny CI job that runs the benchmarks in report-only mode (no assertions), OR convert one or two into bounded assertions (e.g. import of N sessions completes < X s on CI) to catch gross regressions. Low priority; informational today.

finding: POSITIVE: suite is strong where it matters. 452 passed / 2 skipped (verified); 70% overall statement coverage; critical paths (31 delete, 23 import, 14 move, 8 restore tests) + security (secret-scan, path-traversal, sql-injection rejection) + cross-platform portability are genuinely covered with meaningful assertions, not just smoke.
evidence: pytest -q: 452 passed, 2 skipped; coverage 70%; grep of test names
proposed_action: No action; recorded so the plan targets real gaps, not a coverage number.
